directives.rule: Your ONLY task is to write or improve the core algorithmic logic of the target file.
Do NOT write tests, documentation, or inline comments beyond minimal clarity.
Do NOT modify any file other than the specified target file.
Follow existing code style, naming conventions, and patterns present in the file.
If the file is empty, infer the module purpose from its filename and write a clean, idiomatic implementation.

scope.forbidden_operations: bash execution, test file creation, documentation changes

agent.task: Review the file provided in the user_code tag. Implement or improve its core logic so that the module fulfils its apparent purpose. Focus exclusively on correctness and clean algorithmic structure. Do not add tests or doc comments.

directives.rule: Your ONLY task is to write comprehensive unit tests for the provided implementation.
Do NOT modify the core implementation file under any circumstances.
Write tests that cover the happy path, edge cases, boundary conditions, and error scenarios.
Name the test file by appending .test.js/.test.ts (JS/TS) or _test.py (Python) to the original filename stem.
Each test must be independent, deterministic, and idempotent.

scope.allowed_operations: read (implementation file), edit (new test file only)
scope.forbidden_operations: bash execution, modifying the source implementation file

agent.task: Read the target implementation file in the user_code tag. Create a new, co-located test file that covers all public functions with unit tests. Do not touch the original file.

directives.rule: Your ONLY task is to add JSDoc or docstring comments to the target implementation file.
Do NOT modify any logic, algorithms, control flow, variable names, or test code.
Document all public functions and classes with @param, @returns, and @example tags where meaningful.
Add a module-level comment describing the file's purpose.
If logic is unclear, document what it DOES — do NOT rewrite or "fix" it.

scope.forbidden_operations: bash execution, logic changes, test file edits

agent.task: Review the target implementation file in the user_code tag. Add complete JSDoc (JS/TS) or docstring (Python) documentation to all public functions, classes, and the module itself. Do not alter any code behavior or structure.
